statements.text: Federal Reserve Board releases annual audited financial statements. Federal Reserve Board releases annual audited financial statements
statements.summary: Routine annual Fed financial statement release, no market implications

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board and Federal Open Market Committee release economic projections from the March 17-18 FOMC meeting. Federal Reserve Board and Federal Open Market Committee release economic projections from the March 17-18 FOMC meeting
statements.summary: Stale reference to March 17-18 FOMC projections; already digested 2+ weeks ago.

statements.text: Federal Reserve issues FOMC statement. Federal Reserve issues FOMC statement
statements.summary: No substantive content—duplicate header only, zero policy information

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board announces approval of application by FirstSun Capital Bancorp. Federal Reserve Board announces approval of application by FirstSun Capital Bancorp
statements.summary: Routine Fed banking application approval—administrative, non-policy, no market impact

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board announces approval of application by Associated Banc-Corp. Federal Reserve Board announces approval of application by Associated Banc-Corp
statements.summary: Routine Fed regulatory approval for regional bank; no market implication

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of East Cambridge Savings Bank and former employee of United Bank. Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of East Cambridge Savings Bank and former employee of United Bank
statements.summary: Routine Fed enforcement actions against individual former bank employees; negligible market impact

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of First Financial Bank. Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of First Financial Bank
statements.summary: Routine Fed enforcement action against individual employee; no equity market implication

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement actions with Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Ltd., Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Ltd., New York Branch, Standard Chartered PLC, and Standard Chartered Bank. Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement actions with Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Ltd., Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Ltd., New York Branch, Standard Chartered PLC, and Standard Chartered Bank
statements.summary: Fed terminates enforcement actions against ICBC and Standard Chartered; signals regulatory softening on foreign banks

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement action with Wells Fargo. Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement action with Wells Fargo
statements.summary: Wells Fargo enforcement action termination is modestly bullish for financials sector

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board announces it has made the joint findings with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency required for the OCC to approve a request by Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A., for an exemption under section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act. Federal Reserve Board announces it has made the joint findings with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency required for the OCC to approve a request by Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A., for an exemption under section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act
statements.summary: Routine regulatory exemption for Morgan Stanley inter-affiliate transactions—minimal broad equity impact

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board announces approval of application by Fulton Financial Corporation. Federal Reserve Board announces approval of application by Fulton Financial Corporation
statements.summary: Routine Fed approval for Fulton Financial Corporation - minimal equity market impact, regulatory clarity only

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board announces approval of application by Home BancShares. Federal Reserve Board announces approval of application by Home BancShares
statements.summary: Routine Fed regulatory approval of regional bank application; administrative notice with no macroeconomic impact

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board announces approval of notice by CBS Banc-Corp.. Federal Reserve Board announces approval of notice by CBS Banc-Corp.
statements.summary: Routine Fed regulatory approval for regional bank corporate action; no policy or economic signal

statements.text: Following earlier actions to remove reputation risk from its supervision of banks, Federal Reserve Board requests comment on proposal to codify that removal. Following earlier actions to remove reputation risk from its supervision of banks, Federal Reserve Board requests comment on proposal to codify that removal
statements.summary: Fed formalizes lighter bank supervision via comment proposal; bank-friendly signal

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of United Bank. Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of United Bank
statements.summary: Routine Fed enforcement action against former employee; no systemic or market implications

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of Ally Bank and former employee of Regions Bank. Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of Ally Bank and former employee of Regions Bank
statements.summary: Routine Fed enforcement against former bank employees; no policy implications or market significance.

statements.text: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of Equity Bank and former employee of First State Bank of Dongola. Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of Equity Bank and former employee of First State Bank of Dongola
statements.summary: Routine Fed enforcement action against individual former employees at small regional banks; no market implications.

statements.text: Bessent: There will not be a recession in 2026. US economy can grow at least 3.5%. Consumer confidence rising.
statements.summary: Treasury bullish on growth (3.5%+) and no recession—standard admin messaging, mildly equity-supportive but well-telegraphed

statements.text: Gas prices at $4 per gallon but coming down soon. Iran deal will bring oil prices back to normal.
statements.summary: Lower energy prices moderately supportive for equities if realized; Iran deal remains speculative and lacks concrete terms

statements.text: War will continue for another 2-3 weeks. Iran cannot win. The best deal they will ever get is the one on the table now.
statements.summary: Hawkish containment: specific 2-3 week timeline + negotiation framing suggest near-term conflict resolution, moderately bullish in neutral regime.

statements.text: Ordered 100% tariffs on branded pharmaceutical imports. Overhauled steel aluminum and copper duties.
statements.summary: Protectionist pharma and commodity tariffs inflate input costs across healthcare and manufacturing; bearish for margins and consumer prices.

statements.text: Regime change was not the goal but it has occurred because of all their original leaders death.
statements.summary: Post-hoc commentary on completed regime change; explanatory tone suggests market already priced in; geopolitical exposure undefined

statements.text: Not interested in reopening Strait of Hormuz. Allies must find courage to secure it themselves.
statements.summary: US steps back from Strait of Hormuz security, reducing geopolitical guarantees and raising energy supply uncertainty—moderately bearish for risk sentiment.

statements.text: National address: US will hit Iran extremely hard over the next 2-3 weeks. Core strategic objectives nearing completion.
statements.summary: Imminent Iran military action with 2-3 week timeline triggers geopolitical risk premium; broad equity headwind offset partially by defense strength and oil rally.

statements.text: Iran president has just asked the United States of America for a CEASEFIRE!
statements.summary: Iran ceasefire request de-escalates geopolitical risk premium; bullish for equities via reduced oil-supply risk and lower inflation expectations

statements.text: Iran has suffered tremendous damage. Their economy is in ruins. A deal is the only way forward for them.
statements.summary: Sanctions enforcement + negotiation signal modestly reduce geopolitical risk premium

statements.text: Bessent before Senate Finance Committee: Economy showing substantial improvement. Stage set for robust non-inflationary growth. Trump economy back.
statements.summary: Treasury confidence in sustained non-inflationary growth supports near-term risk-on positioning

statements.text: Great progress has been made regarding peace treaty with Iran. Very close to a deal.
statements.summary: Iran peace progress reduces geopolitical risk premium; bullish for oil stability, headwind for defense spending

statements.text: Liberation Day anniversary: all existing tariffs remain in place, new pharmaceutical tariffs coming next week.
statements.summary: Existing tariff regime locked in; new pharmaceutical tariffs announced for next week, creating sector-specific headwinds

statements.text: Iran deal could happen in days not weeks. But if they dont cooperate we will hit them like never before.
statements.summary: Near-term Iran deal timeline with escalation threat creates mixed signals; standard posturing limits novelty

statements.text: Iran must reopen Strait of Hormuz as precondition for any deal. Gas prices will come down when they comply.
statements.summary: Iran negotiations rhetoric; Strait of Hormuz reopening as deal precondition signals both upside (potential energy supply normalization) and downside (ongoing conflict/closure risk)

statements.text: Extending pause on attacking Iran energy facilities by 10 days to April 6. Talks continuing.
statements.summary: De-escalatory 10-day pause reduces geopolitical risk premium on oil; modest tailwind for equities pending April 6 outcome

statements.text: Bessent convenes Financial Stability Oversight Council. Financial system resilient despite war. Monitoring market developments closely.
statements.summary: Treasury affirms financial resilience amid geopolitical uncertainty through routine FSOC oversight

statements.text: Iran negotiations going very well. Could have a deal soon. Markets will love it.
statements.summary: Bullish Iran normalization framing; repeated rhetoric with no concrete timeline or new details

statements.text: Postponing planned strikes on Iran power plants amid productive talks. Great progress being made.
statements.summary: De-escalatory pivot reduces Iran conflict tail risk; bullish for energy stability and risk-on sentiment

statements.text: Powell: Federal Reserve prepared to act as appropriate to support the economy amid geopolitical uncertainty
statements.summary: Fed signals dovish readiness amid geopolitical risks; standard supportive messaging

statements.text: Powell: Dot plot deeply divided — 7 members favor no cuts in 2026, 7 favor one cut, 5 favor two or more.
statements.summary: Fed deeply divided on 2026 cuts (7/7/5 no/one/multiple); policy uncertainty stoking growth headwinds despite slight dovish tilt.

statements.text: Powell: Implications of Middle East developments for US economy are uncertain. Fed prepared to adjust policy as appropriate.
statements.summary: Fed acknowledges geopolitical risk but takes defensive stance with vague policy flexibility; modest headwind to equities.

statements.text: Powell: FOMC holds rates at 3.5-3.75%. Inflation not coming down as much as hoped. Only one rate cut projected for 2026.
statements.summary: Fed holds rates steady with sticky inflation and minimal cuts ahead—hawkish surprise pressures growth equities, supports financials.

statements.text: US launches second wave of strikes hitting Iranian power grid and oil refineries. War entering decisive phase.
statements.summary: Military escalation raises oil prices and geopolitical risk premium; bearish for broad equities, bullish for defense.

statements.text: Countries buying Iranian oil will face 25% secondary tariffs effective immediately.
statements.summary: Secondary tariffs on Iran trade boost energy/defense but create stagflationary cost headwinds for consumer/tech; net slightly bearish in neutral regime

statements.text: If Iran does not come to the table in 48 hours we will strike their energy infrastructure and bring them to the Stone Age.
statements.summary: Iran energy strike threat with 48-hour ultimatum spikes geopolitical risk; energy tailwind offset by risk-off pressure on growth/consumer

statements.text: Iran war will be over very soon. Tremendous progress. They are begging for a deal.
statements.summary: Geopolitical de-escalation rhetoric reduces risk premium, modestly bullish for equities, but lacks concrete deal specifics.

statements.text: Bessent: US economy resilient despite oil disruption. Strategic Petroleum Reserve being deployed. No recession.
statements.summary: Recession denial + active SPR deployment signal policy confidence, bullish for equities despite oil disruption risk
